mappings.rationaleAI: Mating behaviours, reproductive investment asymmetries, and sexual dimorphism address sexual reproduction (A.2), natural selection and reproduction (A.3), adaptive significance of sex (B.3), sex patterns (B.5), and reproductive system adaptations (D.3).
DNA structure and sex linkage address molecular replication (A.1.a), genetic diversity (B.3), and sex chromosomes (B.6.a).
Cell division addresses cell reproduction through fission (A.1.b) and reproduction of organisms (A.2).
Plant pollination and reproduction addresses sexual and asexual reproduction (A.2), general plant reproductive features (C.1), and sex organs in tracheophytes (C.3).
Hormonal regulation of reproductive cycles addresses hormonal influences on sex (B.6.d) and gonads in hormone cycles (D.3.c).
Natural selection addresses reproduction (A.3), adaptive significance of sex (B.3), and origin of sex (B.4).
Animal diversity reveals varied reproductive strategies including life cycles (A.1.d), invertebrate (D.2) and vertebrate reproductive systems (D.3).
Cell division as the basis of development addresses cell reproduction (A.1.b) and reproduction of organisms (A.2).
Fungal reproductive strategies address reproduction of organisms (A.2) and sexlike recombination (B.2).
Binary fission addresses cell reproduction (A.1.b), and genetic exchange relates to transduction and transformation (B.2).
Diverse microbial reproductive strategies address cell reproduction (A.1.b), organism reproduction (A.2), and sexlike recombination (B.2).
Chromosomal theory addresses genetic diversity (B.3) and sex chromosomes (B.6.a).
Reproductive behaviours address mechanisms aiding gamete union in invertebrates (D.2.b) and vertebrate fertilization adaptations (D.3.b).
Social construction of sexual norms addresses the distinctions between sex, sexuality, and reproduction (B.1).

Covers mammalian reproductive strategies, adaptive radiations, and endothermy — directly relevant to reproduction of organisms (unresolved leaf A.1.c), natural selection and reproduction, and vertebrate reproductive systems.
Covers insect development and ecological diversity including reproductive strategies across over one million species — relevant to reproduction of organisms and invertebrate reproductive systems.
Covers amphibian courtship behavior, reproductive strategies, and the tadpole phase — relevant to reproduction of organisms and vertebrate reproductive systems.
Covers DNA structure, gene expression, and cell division mechanisms — relevant to molecular replication and reproduction, and sex chromosomes.
